figures.caption: Fig. 3. Qualitative segmentation comparison on representative axial slices from the 190-slice evaluation set.

Fig. 2. Qualitative generation comparison (random axial slices). From top: real HUpreserved CT, LungDDPM+ [7], MedVAE [12], and DiffSegLung (ours). Our model preserves HU density ranges and parenchymal texture more faithfully than both baselines.
Table 3. Ablation study. Each row enables (✓) or disables (×) individual components. DSC and HD95 are averaged over four classes.

Fig. 1. Training: ϵθ is trained on HU-preserved patches via Ldiff ; radiomic descriptors r ∈ R34 align student embeddings via InfoNCE (discarded at inference). Inference: multi-timestep features fseg are clustered by GMM and refined by Sobel-Diffusion.
